div.div: Verified against code 2026-06-30 (apps/api/src/modules/auth). Single JWT access token, 7-day expiry. No refresh token, no server-side logout endpoint.

Lookup user by sub

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 498px; height: 1px; padding-top: 500px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 10px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Logout = client deletes token locally. No server endpoint exists — JWT is stateless, no blacklist, can't force-revoke a token before its 7-day expiry.

rect.style: stroke: light-dark(rgb(68, 68, 68), rgb(179, 179, 179));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 528px; height: 1px; padding-top: 383px; margin-left: 1332px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Architecture Notes — why JWT over server sessions

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 498px; height: 1px; padding-top: 412px; margin-left: 1342px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
• Stateless — no session store needed; any EC2 instance can validate any request independently.
• Simplifies horizontal scaling — no sticky sessions required for auth; the hot JwtStrategy.validate() lookup is read-through cached in Redis (TTL 30s, fail-open on Redis errors) instead of hitting Postgres every request.
• Same JWT works for both password and OAuth users — one unified identity payload (sub/email/role) regardless of login method.
• Trade-off accepted: can't force-revoke a token before its 7-day expiry (no blacklist) — acceptable at this low-risk, pre-launch stage; refresh-token rotation is a planned hardening step, not yet built.
